title: cerbera odollam
intro: Cerbera odollam, also known as the 'Toddy palm' or 'Sea mango', is a plant native to the coastal regions of the Indian Ocean and Southeast Asia. The plant's toxic seeds, particularly the fruit pulp and the seeds, have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cerbera odollam include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, Cerbera odollam is often used in the form of a paste, oil, or extract, although it is essential to use caution when handling the plant due to its toxic seeds.
